description_long: This object consists of three metal open-ended wrenches, each with a distinct size and shape, arranged upright and leaning against each other. The wrenches are made of steel and display a dark, weathered patina with visible rust and wear, especially along the jaws and handles. The open ends of the wrenches are U-shaped, designed to fit over hexagonal nuts and bolts, and the handles are flat and slightly tapered. The surfaces are rough and matte, with patches of oxidation and discoloration indicating prolonged use and exposure to the elements. No visible brand logos or markings are present on any of the wrenches. The overall appearance suggests these tools have been used extensively in urban or industrial environments.
description: A group of three weathered, rusted steel open-ended wrenches with flat, tapered handles and U-shaped jaws, showing significant wear and patina.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad faces of all three wrenches, highlighting their open jaws and the rusted, worn surfaces.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profiles of the wrenches and their relative thickness, with the handles and jaws aligned edge-on.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the backs of the wrenches, similar to the front but with slightly different angles and visible wear patterns.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profiles of the wrenches from the other edge, emphasizing their flatness and the arrangement of the group.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Significant rust and wear marks are visible across all surfaces, especially on the jaws and handles, with a patchy, uneven distribution.
